title: Why can't Monday.com publish pages like Confluence?
excerpt:
Why can’t Monday.com publish pages like Confluence ?  We need to be able to produce linked documents like Confluence can… and also use pages to index/catalogue requirements documents, specifications, designs etc etc. 
Wh…

posts.author: APLUSMikeG (Michael Green)
posts.cookedHtml:
Why can’t Monday.com publish pages like Confluence ? We need to be able to produce linked documents like Confluence can… and also use pages to index/catalogue requirements documents, specifications, designs etc etc.

Why can’t Monday.com do this basic ‘living document’ and page publishing thing ?

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
I guess Monday.com isn’t designed as a documentation platform like Confluence is. Its core focus is on workflow automation, project and task management, and visual dashboards, not long-form content or structured documentation.

Here’s a breakdown of why this functionality feels missing:

1. Different Core Purposes

  • Monday.com is a Work OS built for planning, tracking, and executing work across teams.
  • Confluence, on the other hand, is a collaboration wiki built for documenting, organizing, and publishing living knowledge (like specs, designs, etc.).

2. Lack of Hierarchical Page Structure

  • Confluence has a native tree structure for pages, which allows linking, indexing, and nesting content for easy navigation.
  • Monday has boards, items, and dashboards, which are flat and data-oriented—not hierarchical content containers.

3. Limited Rich Text + Linking

  • While Monday supports updates and some rich text in Item Updates or Docs, it lacks:
    • Page versioning
    • Deep-linking between documents
    • Embedded indexes or structured navigation
    • Permission layering on a per-page basis

4. Docs Feature is Still Maturing

  • Monday.com Docs is evolving and has collaborative features (like comments, mentions, etc.), but it’s not yet a full competitor to Confluence’s robust publishing system.

Workarounds You Could Explore:

  • Use Monday Docs for light documentation, and organize them with dashboards or link them in items.
  • For deeper document management, consider integrating Confluence with Monday (via automation or Zapier).
  • Some teams use Notion, Google Docs, or ClickUp Docs alongside Monday for this exact reason.

Final Thought:

You’re totally right—if your workflow involves a lot of specs, designs, and living documents, Monday on its own can feel limiting. That’s because it wasn’t originally designed for content publishing. Hopefully, future updates to Monday Docs will close that gap.

posts.author: APLUSMikeG (Michael Green)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for your very detailed and thoughtful answer.

However it does fail to address the big gap between a work management tool and a documentation tool - and the integration between the two. The big advantaage a tool like Jira has over Mondayis its tight integration with Confluence as a tool for specifying, managing and documeting WORK. Any Software Development is NOT just about the mangement of tasks but the DETAIL of those Epics, Stories and Tasks - and that’s where Monday, Monday DEV falls down , is lacking. Looking forward to Monday Docs being fixed,upgraded , getting competetive with Confluence to address this obvious gap…
